Kanye West to return to UK for Wireless
Festival
6 days ago
Share Save Add as preferred on Google
Jasmine Sandhar and Peter Gillibrand, BBC Newsbeat
Getty Images
It will be his first UK performance in over a decade
US rapper Kanye West has been announced as the headliner for all three
nights of this year's Wireless Festival.
sentences.subject: It
sentences.obj: first UK performance a decade US Kanye the all this Wireless

sentences.original:
The controversial star last performed in the UK when he headlined
Glastonbury in 2015.
sentences.subject: The controversial star

sentences.original:
His return to the Wireless stage in London follows years of widespread
criticism for antisemitic comments, which he later publicly apologised
for.
sentences.subject: return the Wireless

sentences.original:
The announcement comes days after the 48-year-old, now known as Ye,
released his twelfth studio album Bully.
sentences.subject: The announcement

sentences.original:
Festival organisers say "Ye's UK comeback will be an extraordinary
chapter in Wireless's story".
sentences.subject: Festival organisers

sentences.original:
Running from 10 to 12 July, the rapper is set to perform tracks from
across his discography when he takes to the stage at Finsbury Park.
sentences.subject: the rapper

sentences.original:
The set list is expected to range from his 2004 debut The College Dropout,
to later albums like 2007's Graduation and 2016's The Life of Pablo.
sentences.subject: The set list

sentences.original: No other acts have been announced on the 2026 line-up yet.
sentences.subject: No other acts

sentences.original:
Getty Images
Ye last headlined a festival in 2024 for Rolling Loud in California
Ye has sparked significant controversy for his antisemitic remarks in
recent years.
sentences.subject: Getty Images Ye

sentences.original:
In 2025, he was blocked from entering Australia after he released a song
glorifying Nazi leader Adolf Hitler.
sentences.subject: he

sentences.original:
Earlier that year, he declared himself a Nazi and retracted an apology for
earlier comments.
sentences.subject: he

sentences.original:
In 2022, sportswear giant Adidas announced it was ending its partnership
with West over his antisemitism.
sentences.subject: giant Adidas

sentences.original:
In November, Ye held a meeting with Rabbi Yoshiyahu Yosef Pinto where
he reportedly apologised for his past antisemitic comments.
sentences.subject: Ye

sentences.original:
Then in January, Ye took out a full-page advert in the Wall Street Journal
where he shared details about his bipolar type-1 diagnosis, which he had
previously dismissed.
sentences.subject: Ye
sentences.obj: a full page advert the Wall Street bipolar type-1

sentences.original:
Within the letter, titled "To Those I've Hurt", he said as a result of the
disorder he had "lost touch with reality" and gravitated towards "the most
destructive symbol I could find, the swastika" and sold t-shirts with the
sign on it.
sentences.subject: he

sentences.original:
He added that it led him to say and do things he regrets and insisted he is
"not a Nazi or an antisemite".
sentences.subject: He

sentences.original:
His new album Bully was delayed numerous times, and at one stage was
due to be released a few days after the advert was published.
sentences.subject: new album

sentences.original:
'Completely unacceptable'
Singer and actor Benjamin Haim-Isaac, who describes himself as a "very
active" member of the Jewish community, says it has been "really horrific"
to see someone offered such a large platform after saying "something so
awful".
sentences.subject: unacceptable Singer actor Benjamin Haim a active the Jewish

sentences.original:
"It really makes you question whether the people who are organising this
festival actually support the Jewish community", the 24-year-old, from
London, tells BBC Newsbeat.

Haim-Isaac, who says he used to love some of Ye's hit songs like
American Boy, says he struggles with the rapper's public apology as he
has gone back on his word in the past.
sentences.subject: Haim Isaac hit American

sentences.original:
While he says he does not support "cancel culture", he is troubled by how
recently Ye said "such horrific things".
sentences.subject: he

sentences.original: "And it wasn't just every now and again, a few antisemitic tropes.
sentences.subject: it

sentences.original:
This
man was a raging, raging antisemite," he says.
sentences.subject: he

sentences.original:
"I think it's really worrying when someone with that big of a platform
says things like that.
sentences.subject: I

sentences.original:
"
A spokesperson for Campaign Against Antisemitism told Newsbeat Ye's
past antisemitic comments have had a "huge impact" and it was
"disappointing" to see venues invite him back so quickly following his
most recent apology.
sentences.subject: A spokesperson Campaign Against

sentences.original:
"If he remains on the right path and makes more effort to make amends,
that is well and good, but if he returns to his old ways these venues will
have much to answer for," they added.
sentences.subject: that

sentences.original:
The Community Security Trust, a charity which provides protection for
Jewish communities in the UK, said booking Ye as a headliner was
"completely unacceptable".
sentences.subject: The Community Security Trust a Jewish the

sentences.original:
"There is little confidence that he will not repeat his appalling views in
future," a spokesperson said.
sentences.subject: a spokesperson

sentences.original:
"Antisemitism causes real harm to Jewish communities and decisions
like this risk signalling that anti-Jewish racism is welcome in the music
industry.
sentences.subject: Antisemitism

sentences.original:
"
Getty Images
The US rapper has slowly been returning to main stages over the past few years
Ye has slowly been returning to main stages, headlining 2024's Rolling
Loud in California with US rapper Ty Dolla $ign and performing live in
China, South Korea and Mexico over the past two years.
sentences.subject: The US rapper

sentences.original:
Last year, Canadian rapper Drake headlined all three days of Wireless
Festival, with each night focused on a different era of his extensive back
catalogue.
sentences.subject: Canadian rapper Drake

sentences.original:
But fans were left disappointed after the 39-year-old's performance on
the final day ended after 40 minutes, when the previous night ran for 90
minutes.
sentences.subject: fans

sentences.original:
There was also mixed reaction from fans when the 2025 Wireless line-up
was announced, due to its lack of UK artists.

Burna Boy, Vybz Kartel, PARTYNEXTDOOR and Summer Walker
performed alongside Drake last year.
sentences.subject: Burna Boy Vybz Summer

sentences.original: About 50,000 rap, R&B and hip-hop fans head to the event each day.
sentences.subject: 50,000 rap hip hop

sentences.original:
A spokesperson for Haringey Council, which manages Finsbury Park, told
BBC Newsbeat that organisers Festival Republic would need to meet
several conditions.
sentences.subject: A spokesperson Haringey Finsbury

sentences.original:
They said this included "ensuring performing acts do not offend or
denigrate any race or religion".
sentences.subject: They

sentences.original:
"We'll be seeking assurances from Festival Republic that they'll be
reminding all artists of this condition prior to them performing this
summer," they added.
sentences.subject: they

sentences.original:
BBC Newsbeat has approached the Wireless organisers for comment on
criticism following Ye being announced as a headliner.
sentences.subject: BBC Newsbeat
